summary: Iteration 61 (Credit-Based Marketplace + Persistent Credit Counter + Custom Top-Ups) frontend e2e testing complete. Navbar CreditCounter, TopUpModal (both tabs incl. validation + quick chips), Marketplace credit price chips, ListingDetail Deploy button, idempotent purchase, DirectPublishModal (price_credits-only), and unauthenticated negative case all PASS. One observation: fresh credit-decrement on first deploy could not be verified because the freeuser already owned the 'Iter61 Test Bot' listing from a prior iteration; the idempotency path ('You already own this agent.' toast + redirect to /my-deployments) was confirmed instead.

integration_issues.issue: Could not validate the 'balance drops by 50 + toast Deployed · -50 cr' assertion because freeuser@test.com already owns the seeded 'Iter61 Test Bot' listing (idempotency kicked in on the very first click — empty toast on first attempt, then explicit 'You already own this agent.' on second). The seed/fixture should reset ownership before this scenario can be retested.

action_items: Reset freeuser@test.com's ownership of the 'Iter61 Test Bot' listing (or seed a fresh listing per test run) so the fresh-purchase decrement can be E2E-verified on the next iteration.

Minor polish: on the first-click 'already-owned' path the toast was empty before redirect; consider firing the same 'You already own this agent.' toast on Marketplace.deployFromCard / ListingDetail Deploy as on the second attempt for consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: credits.jsx polling (30s) + on-focus refresh is solid; refreshCredits() exposed and called after purchase — good.
TopUpModal validation logic (parseFloat, 1<=x<=1000) correctly disables the Buy button for empty/0/1500/out-of-range; receipt '~1,315 credits' for $25 matches the 0.019 rate.
CreditCounter cleanly hides when token is null (unauth) — confirmed on /exchange after localStorage.clear().
DirectPublishModal no longer exposes dp-rent-price / dp-buy-price (USD fields removed). Helper copy 'Suggested: simple 10-50 cr...' and 'You keep 90%' present.

success_rate.frontend: ≈95% (idempotency path verified; fresh-decrement blocked by pre-owned fixture)

test_credentials: admin@nova.ai/admin123 (unlimited, shows ∞), freeuser@test.com/test123 (balance 4,684 at test time)
seed_data_creation: None created; relied on existing 'Iter61 Test Bot' (id 0073a823ff294c1391a6500b231b2e92) priced at 50 cr.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All UI/UX assertions in the review request were verified against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. The single un-verified item is the visual credit-decrement after a *fresh* successful purchase — this requires the buyer to NOT already own the listing. If main agent wants explicit visual proof of decrement, they can either (a) reset listing ownership for freeuser, (b) seed a new listing per run, or (c) test with a different buyer account that doesn't yet own 'Iter61 Test Bot'.
verified_testids: navbar-credit-counter (✓ admin shows ∞, freeuser shows 4,684, hidden when unauth)
agent-price-{id} → 'Free' or 'N cr' chip with NO '$' on /exchange
listing-deploy-btn → 'Deploy · 50 cr' (idempotent: 2nd click shows 'You already own this agent.' toast + redirects to /my-deployments)
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional defect; only an environmental/data-state limitation (freeuser already owns the test listing from a prior iteration), which actually validates the backend's idempotent design.
